title: Microsoft and Chevron plan one of the largest gas-powered data center projects in US
blurb: Microsoft and Chevron announced plans to develop Project Kilby, a 2.67-gigawatt natural gas plant in West Texas dedicated to powering a Microsoft AI and cloud data center. The 20-year power purchase agreement marks a shift for a company pledged to eliminate its carbon emissions by 2030, with the plant projected to release over 13 million tons of carbon dioxide annually.

title: [AINews] Claude Tag: Multiplayer, Proactive, Persistent Agents in Slack
blurb: Anthropic launched Claude Tag, a Slack-native way to delegate work to Claude as an asynchronous teammate that joins channels with access to selected tools, data, and codebases. Tag can be pulled into work threads, loop in coworkers who own related code, wait on git webhooks for blocking dependencies over days, and run in an ambient mode that responds to channels without being explicitly tagged.

title: 오픈AI, 오픈소스 보안 강화 나선다…‘패치 더 플래닛’ 프로젝트 출범
blurb: OpenAI launched Patch the Planet, an AI-driven program built with security firm Trail of Bits that finds and fixes vulnerabilities in widely used open-source software including Python, Go, cURL, and pyca/cryptography. The program pairs AI vulnerability research and Codex Security analysis with human expert review, and has surfaced hundreds of security issues and merged dozens of patches through projects' existing disclosure processes.

title: Build real agentic apps using CUGA: two dozen working examples on a lightweight harness
blurb: IBM released CUGA, an open-source agent harness that handles planning, the execution loop, tool calls, and state management so developers only specify a tool list and a prompt. It plans before acting using a mix of tool calls and generated code (CodeAct), runs a reflection step to catch bad calls and re-plan, and ships with two dozen single-file FastAPI example apps plus configurable Fast, Balanced, and Accurate reasoning modes.

title: Linux Foundation extends DNS to AI agents with new Agent Name Service
blurb: The Linux Foundation announced the Agent Name Service, an open standard that gives AI agents verified identities through the existing DNS rather than a new lookup network or proprietary registry. ANS lets organizations confirm which company an agent belongs to, what it is permitted to do, and whether its code or activity log has been altered, with early backing from GoDaddy, Cloudflare, Cisco, and Salesforce.

title: Dell launches AI server based on Nvidia Vera Rubin GPUs
blurb: Dell launched the PowerEdge XE8812, a liquid-cooled AI server built on Nvidia's Vera Rubin NVL4 architecture that scales to 144 GPUs per rack as the centerpiece of its Dell AI Factory with Nvidia platform. The system expands from 144 to 176 cores and adds 50% more memory per socket and GPU over the prior GB200 generation, letting HPC users run larger models entirely in-memory.

title: Arm servers capture over 45% of data center market revenue — GPU clusters and high-end AI infrastructure fuel a tectonic shift away from x86
blurb: Arm-based servers now command over 45% of data center revenue, according to IDC, as GPU- and ASIC/FPGA-accelerated systems generated more than 70% of global server revenue in Q1 2026. The overall server market hit a record $122.6B for the quarter, up 30.4% year-over-year, with Dell leading at $20.3B in revenue after AI demand drove 244.1% growth.

title: AdversaBench: Automated LLM Red-Teaming with Multi-Judge Confirmation and Cross-Model Transferability
blurb: AdversaBench is an end-to-end LLM red-teaming pipeline that mutates seed prompts with five structured operators, queries a target model, and confirms failures through a three-judge panel with a meta-judge tiebreaker. Across 45 seeds spanning reasoning, instruction-following, and tool use, every seed produced a confirmed failure, and adversarial prompts generated against Llama 3.1 8B transferred zero-shot to Llama 3.3 70B.

title: LaGO: Latent Action Guidance for Online Reinforcement Learning
blurb: This paper proposes LaGO (Latent Action Guidance for Online Reinforcement Learning), a framework that uses a pretrained LLM as a latent action prior to softly guide online policy optimization rather than as a direct controller. On the CLEVR-Robot and Meta-World benchmarks, LaGO raised average success rates over Vanilla PPO from 15.1% to 27.2% and from 2.7% to 15.2% respectively, with stronger LLMs providing more effective guidance.

title: LLM-Driven Feature Discovery
blurb: This post introduces LLM-Driven Feature Discovery, an unsupervised method for surfacing notable behaviors in model transcripts without using model internals. The approach splits transcripts into user turns, thoughts, and responses, asks a black-box autorater to generate 10-20 features per piece, then embeds, clusters, and labels them, functioning as a "black box SAE" that requires just one LLM call per prompt.

title: Detecting AI Coding Agents in Open Source: A Validated Multi-Method Census of 180 Million Repositories
blurb: This paper introduces a multi-method framework for detecting AI coding agents across World of Code's 180M+ Git repositories, combining configuration-file scanning, commit-message analysis, author-identity matching, and bot-signature lookup. Bot-account lookup alone recovers only 3.3% of the 850,157 Claude Code commits found in one snapshot, a 30x relative-recall gap, and a separate pull-request census misses 79% of commit-detected Claude Code adopters, so no single channel is representative.

title: BluTrain: A C++/CUDA Framework for AI Systems
blurb: BluTrain is an architecture-general training framework built from scratch in standard C++ and CUDA, implementing every layer natively including a typed tensor module with reverse-mode autograd, a caching allocator, distributed execution, and an MLIR-based compiler. Training a 124M-parameter GPT-2 baseline in FP32 on 8 6000 Ada GPUs, it sustained 407K tokens/s against PyTorch's 395K while cutting memory footprint by up to 22% and preserving numerical fidelity.

title: The Coming Loop
blurb: The author argues that the real shift in agentic engineering is the "harness loop" that sits outside the coding agent, queuing work, judging whether the model is actually done, and restarting or rerouting tasks to keep them alive past the point where the model would stop. He remains skeptical for code he cares about, citing a loss of control and comprehension. Present-day models, he notes, write code that is too defensive and too local, adding fallbacks instead of making bad states impossible.

title: SpaceX to acquire the AI coding startup Cursor for $60 billion
blurb: SpaceX agreed to acquire AI coding startup Cursor for $60B in stock, a deal expected to close in Q3 pending regulatory approval. The acquisition is meant to bolster SpaceX's frontier AI efforts against Anthropic and OpenAI after its xAI merger, though Cursor's category share has slipped from 41% in June 2025 to about 26% in May.

title: Anthropic ships major Claude Design overhaul with design system imports, code round-trips, and a fix for its token-burning problem
blurb: Anthropic shipped a major overhaul of Claude Design that adds design system imports from GitHub repos and design files, code round-trips, and a fix for the tool's heavy token consumption. Once a design system is imported, Claude builds with those components, checks its output against the system, and auto-corrects to keep generated work on brand and connected to existing enterprise tools.

title: Amazon hopes to challenge Nvidia more directly by selling its AI chips
blurb: Amazon's AI chief Peter DeSantis told Bloomberg that AWS is in early talks to sell its Trainium AI chips to other companies for use in their own data centers, marking a more direct challenge to Nvidia. CEO Andy Jassy has said the chip unit would run at roughly $50B annually as a standalone business, against Nvidia's $326B revenue run rate.

title: CCCL Runtime: A Modern C++ Runtime for CUDA
blurb: NVIDIA introduced CCCL runtime, a set of idiomatic modern C++ APIs that reimplement core CUDA functionality including stream management, memory allocation, and kernel launches through headers like `<cuda/stream>`, `<cuda/buffer>`, and `<cuda/launch>`. It serves as an alternative to the traditional CUDA runtime API, using modern C++ features for safer, composable abstractions while shipping compatibility helpers that let developers adopt it incrementally without rewriting surrounding code.

title: PHANTOM: A Large-Scale Dataset of Multimodal Adversarial Attacks for Vision-Language Models
blurb: PHANTOM is a large-scale open-source dataset of 47,524 pre-generated adversarial attacks against vision-language models, spanning 10 high-level categories and 55 subcategories of harmful intents. Built by consolidating and extending prior benchmarks into 7,826 intents, it lets researchers stress-test VLM robustness, fine-tune attack-generation models, and develop guardrails without bearing the compute cost of generating attacks themselves.

title: Can Scale Save Us From Plasticity Loss in Large Language Models?
blurb: This paper studies plasticity loss, the decline in a network's ability to learn new information, in GPT-style Transformers ranging from 5M to 314M non-embedding parameters trained on a multilingual continual learning task. The authors find that plasticity loss persists across all model sizes and follows a sublinear scaling law, meaning larger models delay but do not prevent it, and the effect appears under stationary training as well as abrupt task changes.

title: Sakana AI releases Fugu Ultra system to rival top AI labs
blurb: Sakana AI released Fugu Ultra, a frontier orchestration model available worldwide through a single OpenAI-compatible API that it positions against Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos Preview. Rather than running as a single model, Fugu Ultra delegates subtasks to a pool of expert LLMs, including instances of itself, and matches or surpasses competitors on coding, reasoning, and scientific benchmarks.

title: A specialized reasoning large language model for accelerating rare disease diagnosis: a randomized AI physician assistance trial
blurb: RaDaR is an open-source 32B-parameter reasoning LLM for rare disease diagnosis, trained on 49,170 real free-text cases and 104,666 synthetic cases and outperforming larger open models including the 671B DeepSeek-R1 across public benchmarks and four external validation centers. In a randomized trial, RaDaR assistance improved physicians' rare-disease diagnostic accuracy by 21.44 percentage points over internet search alone.

title: Anthropic launches Claude Tag, replacing its Slack app with a persistent AI teammate that learns, monitors and works autonomously
blurb: Anthropic launched Claude Tag, a persistent AI teammate embedded in Slack that replaces its existing Claude in Slack app and lets anyone in a channel delegate work by tagging @Claude. The product runs on Claude Opus 4.8, is available in beta for Enterprise and Team customers, and operates as a shared multiplayer agent that builds memory, works asynchronously, and breaks tasks into stages using admin-approved tools.

title: AutoSpec: Safety Rule Evolution for LLM Agents via Inductive Logic Programming
blurb: AutoSpec is a framework that automatically evolves expert-designed safety rules for LLM agents from user safe/unsafe annotations, using inductive logic programming to guide counterexample-driven rule synthesis. Across 291 execution traces spanning code execution and embodied agent domains, it raised rule F1 to 0.98 and 0.93, cut false positives by up to 94%, and converged within 4-5 iterations while keeping rules human-readable.

title: Mistral launches OCR 4 for multilingual document extraction
blurb: Mistral released OCR 4, a document understanding model that extracts structured content with bounding boxes, typed block classification, and per-region confidence scores across 170 languages. The model targets legal, financial, and healthcare workflows, is available via API, Mistral Studio, Amazon SageMaker, and Microsoft Foundry, and can be self-hosted as a single container for data-residency requirements.

title: World Models in Pieces: Structural Certification for General Agents
blurb: This paper proves that general agents cannot be universally capable and that standard worst-case guarantees therefore fail to separate critical bottlenecks from irrelevant failures. The authors introduce structural certification, a transition-local framework that maps bounded goal-conditioned performance to entry-wise guarantees on an agent's internal world model, with algorithms achieving an O(1/n) + O(δ) error bound that localizes where long-horizon planning is reliable.

title: Data Augmentation: A Fourier Analysis Perspective
blurb: This paper develops a Fourier analysis and representation theory framework to determine whether partial data augmentation matches full group augmentation. The authors prove that augmenting with a randomly sampled subset of group elements achieves the same minimax rates as full augmentation, while showing that exact invariance requires averaging over the entire group and cannot be reached by any strict subset on expressive hypothesis spaces.

title: Natural Identifiers for Privacy and Data Audits in Large Language Models
blurb: This paper introduces natural identifiers (NIDs), structured random strings like cryptographic hashes and shortened URLs that already appear in common LLM training data, as a tool for post-hoc privacy audits. Because their format allows unlimited same-distribution strings to be generated, NIDs serve as alternative canaries for differential privacy auditing without retraining and as held-out data for dataset inference without a private non-member set.

title: Cross-Lingual Exploration for Parametric Knowledge
blurb: This paper investigates cross-lingual prompting strategies for surfacing factual knowledge that LLMs store unevenly across languages. Evaluating four dimensions of cross-lingual exploration on benchmarks spanning 17 typologically diverse languages, the authors show the approach improves factual recall and consistency while offering a more compute-efficient Pareto frontier than native-language scaling.

title: RE4: Transformation-aware Imitation of Object Interactions Using Manipulation Modes
blurb: RE4 is an imitation learning framework for object manipulation that swaps end-to-end diffusion and flow models for four interpretable steps: self-supervised pose estimation, manipulation mode-aware demonstration retrieval, a mode-constrained transformation and replan, and rollout. Evaluated on state-based and image-based Push-T and Robomimic benchmarks, it preserves performance while staying interpretable and holds up in adversarial and low-data regimes.

title: Zero-Touch OAuth for MCP
blurb: The Enterprise-Managed Authorization (EMA) extension for the Model Context Protocol is now stable, letting organizations manage MCP server access centrally through their existing identity provider. Instead of per-app OAuth, the client obtains an Identity Assertion JWT Authorization Grant during single sign-on and exchanges it for an access token, giving users zero-touch setup and security teams central policy and audit control. Anthropic, Microsoft, and Okta are among early adopters.

title: Trump says he no longer views Anthropic as a national security threat after G7 meeting with CEO
blurb: Trump said in a pretaped Axios interview that he no longer considers Anthropic a national security threat, reversing the administration's stance after meeting CEO Dario Amodei at the G7 Summit. The shift follows a June 12 Commerce Department directive requiring US approval before foreign nationals access Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models, restrictions Trump said he would consider easing.

title: Solving Inverse Problems of Chaotic Systems with Bidirectional Conditional Flow Matching
blurb: This paper introduces Bidirectional Conditional Flow Matching (Bi-CFM), a method for inferring initial conditions from final states in chaotic systems by learning bidirectional mappings between state distributions to limit exponential error accumulation. On the Lorenz, Circuit, and Lorenz 96 systems, Bi-CFM improves five distribution-level metrics over baselines while running more than two orders of magnitude faster, and a conservation-constrained variant scales to globular cluster observations.

title: OpenAI Expands Daybreak With GPT-5.5-Cyber to Help Defenders Patch Security Flaws
blurb: OpenAI released an improved GPT-5.5-Cyber model to trusted defenders under its Daybreak initiative, calling it its strongest model yet for finding and patching software vulnerabilities across large codebases. The company also updated its Codex Security plugin to triage and patch backlogs at scale, and launched Patch the Planet with Trail of Bits to secure open-source projects including cURL, Python, and the Go project.

title: NatureBench: Can Coding Agents Match the Published SOTA of Nature-Family Papers?
blurb: NatureBench is a cross-discipline benchmark of 90 tasks distilled from peer-reviewed Nature-family papers that tests whether AI coding agents can move past reproduction toward genuine scientific discovery. Across ten frontier agent configurations under a web-search-disabled protocol, the strongest model surpassed published SOTA on only 17.8% of tasks, succeeding mainly by translating problems into familiar supervised prediction rather than inventing new methods.

title: MANGO: Automated Multi-Agent Test Oracle Generation for Vision-Language-Action Models
blurb: MANGO is a multi-agent framework that automatically generates fine-grained test oracles for Vision-Language-Action robotic models from natural-language task descriptions, replacing hand-built symbolic oracles. Collaborating Generator, Assessor, and Judge agents iteratively refine the oracles, and on the LIBERO_10 and RoboCasa benchmarks MANGO detected a comparable number of failures while localizing them and providing richer diagnostic information.

title: No Claude Fable 5? No problem: Sakana achieves frontier performance with new Fugu multi-model, auto synthesis system
blurb: Sakana launched Fugu, a multi-agent orchestration system that exposes a single OpenAI-compatible API and dynamically routes queries across a swappable pool of specialized models. CEO David Ha positioned it as a hedge against vendor lock-in after Anthropic revoked public access to Claude Mythos 5 and Fable 5 under a U.S. export control order, claiming Fugu matches those restricted models on third-party agentic benchmarks.

title: claude-sonnet-5
blurb: A model slug named "claude-sonnet-5" surfaced on an Anthropic partner provider, suggesting the next Sonnet release may be close. The leak is unconfirmed and carries no benchmark, pricing, or availability details, but it points to a possible launch as soon as next week.

title: Nvidia backs AI networking switch silicon startup Upscale in $190M raise
blurb: Upscale AI raised $190M in a Series A-1 round led by Premji Invest, with Nvidia joining the extension round alongside Salesforce Ventures and Temasek. The funding brings the AI networking silicon startup to $500M in total funding at a $2B valuation, supporting its open-standard SkyHammer switch chips built for large-scale, synchronized AI workloads.

title: Enterprise-grade AI image generation in 2 seconds is here: Krea 2 Raw and Turbo available as open weights under custom license
blurb: Krea released its frontier image model Krea 2 as open weights on Hugging Face, in Raw and Turbo versions under a custom license that charges firms with more than 50 seats for enterprise use and mandates safeguards against illegal content. Krea 2 Turbo generates images in 2 seconds, among the fastest available, while the company claims it offers more visual variety and customization than typical proprietary or open models.

title: Anthropic Customer Sues US Over Loss of Fable AI Access
blurb: Legion, a US-based litigation-technology company, sued the federal government after Anthropic disabled its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models in response to a Trump administration export control. The order bars the maker of Claude from giving foreign nationals access to its most advanced technology, which Legion says cut off its Canadian software developers who work remotely from Canada.

title: AI Coding Agent Horror Stories: The 13-Hour AWS Outage
blurb: This Docker post recounts a coding-agent failure in which an AI agent's unchecked actions triggered a 13-hour AWS outage. It uses the incident to argue for stronger guardrails around autonomous agents, including sandboxed execution, scoped permissions, and human review before agents touch production infrastructure.

title: AI's Affordability Crisis
blurb: The author argues that AI coding platforms are running an unsustainable subsidy, citing SemiAnalysis tests where a $200-a-month subscription can burn $8,000 in Anthropic tokens or $14,000 in OpenAI tokens. By that math, Anthropic subsidizes some customers up to 40 times and OpenAI up to 70 times, costing the platforms roughly $8 to $14 to generate $1 in revenue. Notably, OpenAI's larger subsidy hasn't paid off: its enterprise adoption is flat while Anthropic's has surged.

title: Patch the Planet: a Daybreak initiative to support open source maintainers
blurb: OpenAI introduced Patch the Planet, a Daybreak initiative built with Trail of Bits that pairs AI-assisted security research from its most cyber-capable models with expert human review to find and patch vulnerabilities in critical open-source projects. Initial participants include cURL, Python, the Go project, pyca/cryptography, Sigstore, and aiohttp, with HackerOne and Calif supporting triage and coordinated disclosure.

title: Codex-maxxing for long-running work
blurb: This OpenAI whitepaper by Jason Liu lays out strategies for using Codex as a persistent workspace for projects that run past a single prompt. It covers breaking ambitious goals into verifiable steps, preserving context and continuity across workstreams, and deciding when to delegate execution to Codex versus when human oversight matters most.

title: Experimenting with the proposed Cross-Origin Storage API in Transformers.js
blurb: This post experiments with the proposed Cross-Origin Storage (COS) API to let Transformers.js share cached model and Wasm resources across different origins in the browser. The current Cache API scopes storage per origin, so visiting two apps that use the same model, like Whisper-tiny.en, forces redundant downloads that add up to 177 MB for a single model in the toy example.

title: Texas lassoes massive Microsoft datacenter - and 20 years of gas turbine emissions
blurb: Texas approved a large Microsoft datacenter that will be powered by on-site gas turbines permitted to emit at high levels for the next 20 years. The arrangement lets the facility sidestep grid constraints by generating its own power, but locks in two decades of fossil-fuel emissions to feed rising AI compute demand.

title: Verifiable, private AI: Google Cloud expands Confidential Computing frontiers
blurb: Google Cloud expanded its Confidential Computing lineup with Confidential G4 VMs running NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell GPUs, now in preview across every region where the standard G4 is available. The instances use AMD SEV on 5th Gen EPYC Turin CPUs to keep AI inference and fine-tuning data encrypted inside hardware-based trusted execution environments, including in transit between CPU and GPU.

title: Algorithmic Monocultures in Hiring
blurb: A Stanford study tracked 3.4 million applicants across 4 million applications screened by a single third-party AI hiring vendor, finding that 26% of Black and 15% of Asian applicants faced positions where the tool recommended their group at below the EEOC's four-fifths threshold. Because the same algorithm screens for many employers, rejected candidates get shut out everywhere they apply, costing an estimated 40,000 advancements.

title: Towards a world where no one is surprised by a natural disaster
blurb: Google shared its crisis resilience research at the AI for the Planet event, detailing progress in AI-based forecasting for floods, cyclones, wildfires, and earthquakes. Flood Hub now covers 2 billion people across more than 150 countries, with river forecasts up to seven days out and new urban flash flood predictions providing up to 24-hour advance notice.

title: A Fair Evaluation of Graph Foundation Models for Node Property Prediction
blurb: This paper reevaluates 9 recent Graph Foundation Models for node property prediction against well-tuned Graph Neural Network baselines under a single fair protocol, addressing a field that has lacked a unified evaluation setting. Only the newest GFMs based on the Prior-data Fitted Networks paradigm beat strong GNNs on predictive performance, and they do so at a higher inference cost.

title: SK Hynix says it is seeking to raise ~$29.4B in a US listing, expects trading to start on July 10, and intends to use the funds for building additional capacity (Bloomberg)
blurb: SK Hynix is seeking to raise about $29.4B through a US listing, with trading expected to begin on July 10. The company plans to use the proceeds to build additional manufacturing capacity, expanding output as demand for AI memory chips continues to climb.

title: SpaceX Strikes $6 Billion Deal With AI Startup for Data-Center Space
blurb: SpaceX struck a $6B deal to rent data-center capacity to Reflection AI, the startup building open-source models. The agreement marks a notable expansion of the newly public company's infrastructure business and gives Reflection the compute it needs to train and develop its models at scale.

title: Nvidia unveils Vera Rubin platform targeting AI, HPC infrastructure
blurb: Nvidia launched the Vera Rubin platform at ISC High Performance 2026, a rack-scale supercomputer combining Vera CPUs, Rubin GPUs, NVLink-C2C interconnects, ConnectX-9 SuperNICs, and BlueField-4 DPUs for AI and scientific HPC workloads. A fully configured system supports up to 144 GPUs per rack and delivers over seven exaflops of AI performance alongside five petaflops of native FP64 compute, with 2.8 times the memory bandwidth of Blackwell.

title: After betting the firm on Anthropic, Menlo Ventures raises victorious $3B fund
blurb: Menlo Ventures raised $3B across new funds, the largest haul in its 50-year history and driven largely by its AI portfolio, led by Anthropic. The firm's stake in Anthropic is now worth roughly $14 billion, stemming from a 2024 bet-the-firm $750M Series D investment that it structured partly as a $500M special purpose vehicle.

title: Micron invests in Anthropic and grants it a supply deal
blurb: Micron made a strategic investment in Anthropic's Series H round and signed a multi-year supply agreement giving the lab allocations of Micron HBM, DRAM, and SSDs. Samsung and SK Hynix also joined the round, and the deal includes joint work on memory and storage performance plus Micron's own adoption of Claude across engineering and manufacturing.

title: AI creative tools startup Krea, which has raised $83M and claims to have 30M users, releases the open weights for its image model Krea 2 under a custom license (Carl Franzen/VentureBeat)
blurb: Krea released the open weights for its image model Krea 2 under a custom license. The startup, which has raised $83M and claims 30M users, is positioning the open release to compete in the increasingly crowded field of open-weight image generators.

title: SK Hynix overtaking Samsung to become South Korea's most valuable company is the culmination of 14 years of bets on HBM, which paid off as the AI boom took off (Reuters)
blurb: SK Hynix overtook Samsung to become South Korea's most valuable company, capping a 14-year bet on high-bandwidth memory that paid off as AI demand surged. HBM, the specialized memory stacked alongside GPUs to feed AI accelerators, has become a critical bottleneck in the AI supply chain, positioning SK Hynix as a primary supplier to Nvidia.

title: Meta launches AI glasses with three new styles from $299
blurb: Meta launched Meta Glasses, a new line of AI eyewear built with EssilorLuxottica, in three frame styles starting at $299: Meta Adventurer, Meta Fury, and a Kylie Jenner collaboration. The glasses run Meta AI on the new Muse Spark model, adding 14 live translation languages, hands-free capture, open-ear audio, and over 8 hours of battery life.

title: Anthropic Backer Menlo Ventures Raises $3B In New Funds To Back AI Startups Across Stages
blurb: Menlo Ventures raised $3B to back AI startups across enterprise, healthcare, and consumer sectors, the largest raise in the firm's 50-year history. The capital is split into Menlo Ventures XVII for seed and Series A deals and Menlo Inflection IV for growth-stage companies, building on the firm's early backing of Anthropic, which has filed for a 2026 IPO targeting $1T or more.

title: Reasoning as Attractor Dynamics: Latent Memory Retrieval via Gibbs-Weighted Energy Minimization
blurb: This paper reframes LLM inference as settling into attractor basins of a Dense Associative Memory rather than greedy next-token prediction, treating correct reasoning chains as deep, wide energy minima and hallucinations as sharp, unstable ones. By sampling multiple reasoning paths and weighting them by inverse energy via a Gibbs measure, the method lifted Microsoft Phi-3.5 on GSM8K from 84.7% to 90.1%.

title: Are We Ready For An Agent-Native Memory System?
blurb: This paper presents a systematic study of LLM agent memory from a data management perspective, decomposing it into four modules (representation and storage, extraction, retrieval and routing, and maintenance) and evaluating 12 memory systems plus two baselines across 11 datasets. The authors find that no single architecture dominates, effectiveness depends on aligning memory structure with the workload bottleneck, and localized maintenance is more cost-efficient than global reorganization.

title: MEMPROBE: Probing Long-Term Agent Memory via Hidden User-State Recovery
blurb: MEMPROBE is a benchmark that evaluates long-term agent memory directly, reconstructing a hidden, taxonomy-anchored user state from the artifact an agent leaves behind rather than judging it through downstream task success. Testing 5 memory systems across 50 simulated users with 31 hidden dimensions each, the authors find task completion nearly saturates while category-balanced recovery stays around 0.6 and drops further under top-k retrieval.

title: ParaPairAudioBench: Paralinguistic Pairwise Audio Benchmark for LALM-as-a-Judge
blurb: ParaPairAudioBench is a pairwise benchmark of 5,175 audio pairs across five paralinguistic dimensions, style, rate, emphasis, age, and gender, built to test how reliably Large Audio-Language Models judge fine-grained speech distinctions. The authors find current LALM judges trail human judgments by 32 percentage points on average and fail badly at calibration, especially in Tie cases where the correct move is to abstain.

title: CORE-BREW: LLR-Based Soft Decoding for Robust Multi-Bit LLM Watermarking
blurb: CORE-BREW is a multi-bit watermarking method for LLM outputs that adds soft-decision decoding by calibrating the watermark channel to a fixed hit rate, producing closed-form per-token log-likelihood ratios. It supports a Strict-Safe mode that preserves bounded-distance acceptance and an FPR-Calibrated mode using likelihood scoring and list decoding, improving low-FPR robustness under token edits and paraphrasing while keeping comparable semantic quality.

title: Sources: prices for Nvidia's AI chips on China's black market have more than doubled amid a US export crackdown; its flagship DGX B300 server has risen to $1.1M (Eleanor Olcott/Financial Times)
blurb: Prices for Nvidia's AI chips on China's black market have more than doubled following a tightened US export crackdown, with the flagship DGX B300 server now selling for roughly $1.1M. The surge underscores persistent Chinese demand for restricted hardware despite Washington's controls, with smuggled systems commanding steep premiums over their official list prices.

title: NSF slashes research programs to support new tech initiative, insiders say
blurb: The National Science Foundation is cutting funding for existing research programs to redirect resources toward a new technology initiative, according to insiders. The reported shift would prioritize emerging tech areas at the expense of established grants, raising concerns among researchers about the stability of long-term funding across the broader science community.

title: Google Gemini co-lead Noam Shazeer to join IPO-bound OpenAI
blurb: Noam Shazeer, co-lead of Google's Gemini and co-author of the original Transformer paper, is leaving Google to join OpenAI as the company moves toward an IPO. His departure removes a central architect of Gemini from Google's roster and adds a foundational researcher to OpenAI ahead of its public offering.

title: Server-Side Tools Are Now Available for DigitalOcean Inference Engine
blurb: DigitalOcean released Server-Side Tools in Public Preview for its Inference Engine, letting a model call web search, web fetch, Knowledge Bases, MCP servers, and supported Anthropic and OpenAI tools from inside a single inference request. The tools work with an existing Model Access Key, with no separate tool infrastructure or orchestration layer to operate, and web search and fetch are powered by Exa under usage-based pricing.

title: Real-Time Interactive Music Generation via Data-Free Streaming Consistency Distillation
blurb: This paper introduces a framework for real-time interactive music generation that distills text-to-music models into single-step streaming generators using a data-free, prompt-only approach that synthesizes teacher-guided trajectories on the fly. Music-aware consistency objectives combining latent, spectral, and temporal-difference losses preserve timbre, transients, and rhythmic stability, letting users steer the musical output live without interrupting the audio stream.

title: Unified Dominance Graph for Interval-Predicate Approximate Nearest Neighbor Search
blurb: This paper introduces the Unified Dominance Graph (UDG), a graph-indexing framework for approximate nearest neighbor search where object validity depends on interval predicates like containment and overlap, common in temporal databases, financial analysis, and RAG. UDG maps interval endpoints into a normalized two-dimensional dominance space so that different predicates reuse the same index and search algorithm, outperforming existing hybrid search baselines while keeping indexing overhead low.

title: AI memory startup focused on cutting token costs raises $98 million
blurb: Engram raised $98M from investors including General Catalyst, Kleiner Perkins, Sequoia, and Andrej Karpathy to build a "learned memory" layer that stores organization-specific workflows and context for cheaper AI output. The 8-month-old startup claims its models can match or outperform frontier labs using up to 100 times fewer tokens, and already counts Microsoft, Notion, and Harvey as clients.

title: Alphabet has its worst day in over a year on AI concerns after high-profile exits
blurb: Alphabet shares closed down about 5% on Monday, its worst day in over a year, as two prominent researchers left for rivals and AI commoditization fears grew. Gemini co-lead Noam Shazeer departed for OpenAI and AlphaFold co-creator John Jumper left for Anthropic, raising questions about whether Google's $141B in AI spending since October is buying a durable advantage.

title: SpaceX signs computing power deal with open-source AI startup Reflection worth up to $6.3 billion
blurb: SpaceX signed a computing power deal worth up to $6.3 billion with open-source AI startup Reflection, giving the lab immediate access to Nvidia GB300 chips on its Colossus infrastructure. Reflection, last valued at $25 billion, will pay $150 million per month from July 2026 through 2029, joining Anthropic, Google, and Cursor as outside customers of SpaceX's data center build-out.

title: Researchers introduce Self-Harness, a framework that lets AI agents rewrite their own rules, boosting performance up to 60%
blurb: Researchers at the Shanghai Artificial Intelligence Laboratory introduced Self-Harness, a framework in which an LLM-based agent improves its own operating rules by examining its execution traces and applying empirical edits rather than manual debugging. The approach targets harness components like system prompts, verification rules, and failure-recovery procedures, and reported performance gains of up to 60% on model-specific weaknesses.

title: Are Safety Guarantees in Neural Networks Safe? How to Compute Trustworthy Robustness Certifications
blurb: This paper introduces the apothem measure for certifying neural network robustness, computing apothem-optimal certifications in a linear number of verifier calls relative to the input domain's diameter. The authors prove volume-optimal certifications are intractable for oracle-based algorithms, add dual certifications that bound all instances of a class, and report at least two-fold improvement over prior work on MNIST and Fashion MNIST via their ParallelepipedoNN system.

title: Okta expands Cross App Access ecosystem to secure AI agent connections
blurb: Okta said more than 25 software makers, including Anthropic's Claude, Slack, Atlassian, Cloudflare, and Microsoft's Visual Studio Code, have joined its Cross App Access framework, which routes AI agent connections to enterprise apps through a company's identity controls. Built as an OAuth extension, XAA now serves as the official authorization extension for the Model Context Protocol, replacing static API keys and unmonitored consent screens with identity policy that follows an agent across applications.

title: Upbound open-sources Modelplane to optimize inference clusters
blurb: Upbound released Modelplane, an open-source tool for managing AI inference clusters built on its Crossplane infrastructure engine. Modelplane spreads inference workloads across multiple clouds, automatically spins up replicas as request volume rises, and caches model weights on local storage to cut the latency of loading them from remote systems.

title: Claude can now join your Slack channels and work alongside your team
blurb: Anthropic released Claude Tag, which adds Claude directly to Slack channels as a shared participant that teams can summon with @Claude to analyze data, write reports, review code, or investigate incidents. Available to Claude Enterprise and Team subscribers, it lets all channel members interact with a single persistent instance, with administrators controlling tool access, data isolation, and monthly spending limits.

title: Latitude launches open-source platform to monitor AI agents
blurb: Latitude released an open-source platform for monitoring AI agents in production, combining a discovery layer that clusters thousands of live conversations, a signals layer that collapses repeated failures into a single actionable alert, and an MCP server that routes fixes back into the coding agent. It runs on a team's own infrastructure under an MIT license, connects to Claude Code to track token spend per task, and can turn production conversations into reusable test sets.

title: China takes back top spot in latest supercomputer ranking
blurb: China's LineShine supercomputer took the world's fastest crown at 2.198 exaflops, beating the US El Capitan system (1.809 exaflops) for the top spot China last held in 2017. The machine sidesteps GPU export embargoes entirely, running 13.79 million cores across a custom 304-core CPU linked by a proprietary interconnect.

title: Nvidia tops Ethernet data center switch charts by revenue as sales soar
blurb: Nvidia became the top data center Ethernet switch vendor by revenue in Q1 2026, with sales up 192.7% year over year to $2.1B for a 21.5% segment share, per IDC's Quarterly Ethernet Switch Tracker. Demand for its Spectrum-X platform from hyperscalers building AI factories drove the gains, though Cisco still leads overall Ethernet switching with a 29.3% share.

title: Humanoid robot maker Agility plans to go public via a merger with Michael Klein's SPAC, in a deal valuing it at ~$2.5B and listing under ticker symbol AGLT (Lauren Thomas/Wall Street Journal)
blurb: Agility Robotics agreed to go public through a merger with Michael Klein's special-purpose acquisition company, in a deal valuing the humanoid robot maker at roughly $2.5B. The combined company will list under the ticker symbol AGLT, giving Agility public-market capital as it scales production of its Digit warehouse robot.

title: Superhuman acquires AI detection startup GPTZero, which has 19M+ registered users and $30M in annual recurring revenue; PitchBook: GPTZero is valued at $88M+ (Geoff Weiss/Business Insider)
blurb: Superhuman acquired GPTZero, the AI detection startup with more than 19M registered users and $30M in annual recurring revenue. PitchBook values GPTZero at over $88M, marking a move by the email company to expand beyond its core product into AI content verification.

title: SK Hynix Seeks $29 Billion With US Listing to Fund AI Boom
blurb: SK Hynix plans to raise 45.45 trillion won ($29.4 billion) in a US listing to fund expansion across its AI memory chip business. The deal could rank among the top five share sales ever, comparable to Saudi Aramco's record 2019 IPO, as supply-chain firms race to add capacity for surging memory demand.

title: Inside Claude Managed Agents: Reverse Engineering the Security Boundaries of Anthropic's Hosted Agent Runtime
blurb: Pluto Security reverse-engineered Claude Managed Agents, Anthropic's hosted runtime where Claude runs autonomously in cloud containers with bash, file I/O, web access, and MCP connections. The analysis found gVisor isolation with three-layer egress control and a vault proxy that keeps secrets out of the sandbox, but also a readable egress JWT and an allowlist silently expanded with six Anthropic hosts beyond what users configure.

title: Midjourney, the AI image generator, is developing a full-body ultrasonic scanner
blurb: Midjourney, known for its text-to-image generator, announced Midjourney Medical and a first hardware product, the Scanner, a full-body ultrasonic machine built with Butterfly Network that aims to produce MRI-quality 3D body maps in under 60 seconds. The company plans to operate spa locations housing the scanners, and will spend the next 12 months running research trials and refining the algorithms and second-generation hardware.

title: Mistral AI to get Code and Apps features on Vibe
blurb: Mistral AI is building Code and Apps sections into the web version of Le Chat (Vibe), adding a browser coding surface that mirrors its CLI agents and an artifacts-style area for building, hosting, and sharing apps via connectors and multi-step workflows. Both features remain in development as CEO Arthur Mensch confirmed a large, sparse mixture-of-experts model shipping this summer as open weights, with partner early access in July.

title: Don't rely on instructions, use Agent Hooks to enforce guardrails
blurb: This post argues that AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md instructions are unreliable guardrails because agents sometimes ignore them, and recommends Agent Hooks for deterministic enforcement instead. It walks through two Claude Code examples, a PreToolUse hook that blocks raw <input> tags before the tool runs and a stop hook that prevents the agent from declaring done while a design-system ratchet test fails, parsing the JSON each hook receives on stdin with jq.

title: Solving an ARD problem in AI: Agentic Resource Discovery
blurb: Google, Microsoft, Cisco, Nvidia, Salesforce, and others proposed Agentic Resource Discovery (ARD), an open protocol that lets AI agents find and safely use the tools they need within a corporate domain. ARD works across two layers: organizations publish catalogs of available capabilities, and registries crawl those catalogs like a search engine. The specification is available now.

title: Atlassian's DESIGN.md is Here: What We Learned Testing Portable Design Context in Practice
blurb: Atlassian tested Google's open-source DESIGN.md format and found its portability trades off against the sophistication of dedicated design-context tooling.

title: World model maker Odyssey nabs $1.45B valuation backed by Amazon and other big names
blurb: Odyssey, a world model startup founded by self-driving veterans Oliver Cameron and Jeff Hawke, raised a $310M Series B at a $1.45B valuation led by Natural Capital, with Amazon, AMD Ventures, and GV participating. As part of the round, Odyssey named AWS its preferred cloud provider and will optimize its models, which generate interactive video from text prompts, to run on AWS Trainium chips.

title: Alibaba's AI video model rises to No. 2 in global rankings, as OpenAI's Sora and ByteDance's Seedance fall away
blurb: Alibaba Cloud released HappyHorse 1.1, an API-first video generation model now live on Model Studio with a 40% launch discount, after the previous version reached No. 2 across all three Artificial Analysis Video Arena leaderboards. The upgrade lands as competitors retreat, with OpenAI discontinuing Sora over costs and ByteDance shelving Seedance 2.0's international rollout amid Hollywood copyright complaints.

title: Google DeepMind and A24 announce first-of-its-kind research partnership
blurb: Google DeepMind and A24 announced a research partnership pairing the lab with the film studio to develop new AI tools and workflows for filmmakers, alongside an undisclosed Google investment in A24. The collaboration spans multiple projects over time, embedding DeepMind's models directly in A24's creative process so its artists can shape the technology while giving researchers feedback from working filmmakers.

title: iOS 27 beta 2 includes Write with Siri, the new version of Apple Intelligence writing tools
blurb: iOS 27 beta 2 replaces Apple Intelligence's Writing Tools with Write with Siri, a natural-language system for generating, proofreading, and rewriting text.

title: Sakana Fugu Launches With 93.2 LiveCodeBench Score After Claude Ban
blurb: Sakana AI released Fugu and Fugu Ultra, a multi-agent orchestration system sold as a single API that routes work across a swappable pool of models. Fugu Ultra scored 93.2 on LiveCodeBench, ahead of Claude Fable 5's 89.8, and the company pitched the service as a way to route around vendor restrictions after a US government directive forced Anthropic to suspend access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5.

title: SpaceX lands $6.3B compute deal with open-source AI startup Reflection and Nvidia is on both sides of the trade
blurb: Reflection AI signed a compute deal with SpaceX worth up to $6.3B, paying $150M a month from July 2026 through 2029 for access to Nvidia GB300 chips at the Colossus 2 data center in Memphis. The agreement makes Reflection the fourth major Colossus client after Anthropic, Google, and Cursor, pushing SpaceX's committed outside compute revenue past $80B through 2029.

title: Legion LegalTech sues US over Anthropic Fable 5 and Mythos 5 shutdown
blurb: Legion LegalTech sued the US federal government over a June 12 Commerce Department directive that forced Anthropic to disable Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for all users worldwide. The San Jose legal-software company, which relies on Anthropic's models and employs Canadian nationals targeted by the export order, called the harm "immediate, irreparable, and existential" and is seeking to vacate the order and block its enforcement.

title: Model selection with proper scoring rules on data sets of time series
blurb: This paper examines how to select between probabilistic time-series models using proper scoring rules, showing that mean score, median score, and mean rank can produce conflicting decisions because of skew in the score distribution. The authors find that all three criteria converge as test sets grow, but only the mean score identifies the true model on short test sets, which they demonstrate on intermittent series including the M5 competition data.

title: Local Qwen isn't a worse Opus, it's a different tool
blurb: The author argues that local Qwen models aren't a cheaper substitute for Opus but a distinct tool with a different cost-benefit profile, drawing on real numbers from running a bootstrapped software business. An RTX 6000 Pro card paid for itself within two or three months on his specific Go and infrastructure workloads, but Qwen still can't be trusted unsupervised. Its worst failure modes, infinite loops and hallucination, surface most once quantized down to fit a consumer GPU.

title: Google Cloud publishes a secure MCP-on-GKE guide
blurb: Google Cloud published a guide for deploying a secure remote MCP server on Google Kubernetes Engine using the Streamable HTTP transport, which lets one stateless server handle multiple client connections over a single HTTP endpoint. The walkthrough centers on GKE Autopilot for horizontal scaling and the Kubernetes Gateway API with SSL certificates to enforce encrypted, authorized-only traffic to a centrally shared server.

title: OpenAI prepares GPT-5.6 models for the upcoming release
blurb: OpenAI is preparing to release its GPT-5.6 family as soon as Tuesday, with standard, Mini, and Pro variants reportedly landing together and a context window pushed toward 1.5 million tokens, up from GPT-5.5's million. Developers testing early builds claim it edges out Anthropic's Mythos line on agentic coding, and OpenAI is said to be planning deeper price cuts on tokens to widen its existing lead.

title: Snowflake and the Agentic Resource Discovery Specification
blurb: Snowflake added support for the Agentic Resource Discovery specification, an open protocol with Microsoft and GoDaddy that standardizes how enterprises catalog and discover AI agents.

title: Build an AI Scientist for Life Science Discovery with NVIDIA BioNeMo Agent Toolkit
blurb: NVIDIA BioNeMo turns its accelerated digital-biology stack into a set of callable tools that AI agents can use for tasks like structure prediction, molecular docking, sequence design, and genomics. BioNeMo Skills package each model with documented inputs, parameters, expected artifacts, and failure modes, while MCP server wrappers expose open models that aren't yet available as NIM through the same agent-callable interface.

title: Beyond U-Net: A Latent-Representation-Aligned Skip-Free Backbone for Flow-Matching Speech Enhancement
blurb: This paper introduces a skip-free encoder-decoder backbone for flow-matching speech enhancement that replaces U-Net skip connections, which can pass noise-correlated features to the decoder, with Latent Representation Alignment against clean features from a frozen Descript Audio Codec. On WSJ0-CHiME3 and VoiceBank-DEMAND, the model improves PESQ and perceptual quality using only five function evaluations.

title: Cerebras falls 10% after chipmaker forecasts shrinking margin in first earnings report since IPO
blurb: Cerebras reported its first earnings since last month's IPO, with revenue nearly doubling to $193.4 million, up 92% year-over-year, against a narrowed $14 million net loss. The stock fell 10% in extended trading after the company guided core gross margin down to 36-38% in Q2 from 46.5% in Q1, even as it projected full-year core revenue growth of roughly 69%.

title: Real vs. Complex Spectral Bases for Neural Operators: The Role of Green's Function Alignment
blurb: This paper introduces the Hartley Neural Operator (HNO), a real-valued mirror of the Fourier Neural Operator that swaps the complex FFT for the Discrete Hartley Transform and learns a single real multiplier per spectral mode. The authors show that HNO wins on self-adjoint elliptic operators like Poisson and biharmonic, while FNO stays ahead on phase-carrying time-dependent operators, giving a predictive rule: match the spectral basis to the symmetry of the solution operator.

title: To Compare, or Not to Compare: On Methodological Practices in Evaluating Social Bias
blurb: This paper introduces a unified framework that standardizes heterogeneous social-bias benchmarks to contrast isolated demographic assessments with forced-choice comparative settings across multiple model families. The authors find that comparative settings act as aggressive catalysts for latent discrimination, that Chain-of-Thought reasoning worsens bias and persists even with neutral fallback options, and that the effect scales positively with model size.

title: Task Decomposition for Efficient Annotation
blurb: This paper proposes decomposing structured annotation tasks into sub-tasks to cut the aggregate inferential load when mixing human and model annotators of varying expertise. The authors introduce a formal model of inferential load based on degrees of freedom in the annotation space, drawn from centering theory, and give guidelines for splitting tasks and allocating sub-tasks across annotators to maximize quality under a fixed budget.

title: Parallel Manifold Steering: Efficient Adaptation of Large Associative Memories via Residual Energy Shaping
blurb: This paper introduces H-Res (Hierarchical Residual Steering), a method for adapting frozen Transformer models that treats fine-tuning as a control problem on the activation manifold, learning a state-dependent vector field that steers token trajectories into task-specific attractor basins without changing weights or adding prompt tokens. The authors report that it outperforms global weight modification like LoRA by 26% on associative retrieval tasks while avoiding the overhead of prompt-based methods.

title: Grading the Grader: Lessons from Evaluating an Agentic Data Analysis System
blurb: This paper investigates how reliably automated graders assess agentic data analysis systems by running LAMBDA on 153 numerical QRData tasks and building a three-layer cascade of strict regex matching, LLM-based lenient grading, and human snippet inspection. Both automated graders hit 100% precision with no false positives, and an iterative nudge mechanism that cues an answer template raised grading run success from 36% to 97%.

title: AGORA: An Archive-Grounded Benchmark for Agentic Workplace Document Reasoning
blurb: AGORA is an archive-grounded benchmark that tests whether LLM agents can locate sparse evidence across large, messy collections of workplace documents, reconciling inconsistent terminology, units, and time conventions to compute answers. It pairs 362 questions with eight domain collections totaling 9,664 authentic documents and 372M tokens, far exceeding any model's context window, and the strongest of eight evaluated models reached only 59.4% accuracy.

title: Give GitHub Copilot CLI real code intelligence with language servers
blurb: This post explains how to install and configure Language Server Protocol (LSP) servers for GitHub Copilot CLI, replacing the agent's brute-force grep and bytecode-extraction workarounds with structured code intelligence. An LSP Setup skill automates server installation across 14 languages, giving the agent precise go-to-definition, find-references, and type resolution instead of text-search heuristics that miss generics, overloads, and compiled code.

title: Four Ways We're Using Our MCP Server at Figma
blurb: Figma shared four internal workflows its teams run with the Figma MCP server, including refreshing living decks and shipping designs to production.

title: We built a persistent agent memory layer on Elasticsearch with 0.89 recall
blurb: Elastic's Search Labs detailed an agent memory layer built on Elasticsearch using three indices structured around cognitive-science memory categories, with hybrid recall via RRF and a cross-encoder reranker, plus supersession for contradictions, decay, and per-user document-level security. On a QA-style evaluation of 168 questions, the system averaged 0.89 recall at 10 with zero cross-tenant leaks.

title: Announcing Stack Overflow for Agents
blurb: Stack Overflow introduced Stack Overflow for Agents, an API-first knowledge exchange that lets AI coding agents share verified solutions instead of working in isolation. The beta targets what the company calls the Ephemeral Intelligence Gap, where agents repeatedly burn compute and tokens rediscovering the same bug fixes and architectural patterns that other agents have already solved.

title: Anthropic says Claude may want to see your ID
blurb: Anthropic updated its privacy policy to allow asking flagged users to verify their age or identity by uploading a government-issued ID, a selfie, and a face geometry template. The company says the requirement, effective July 8, applies only to a small subset of accounts flagged for potential fraud and gives them a path to appeal rather than face an outright ban.

title: Tesla plans to sell modular AI data center hardware called ‘Megapod'
blurb: Tesla filed an intent-to-use trademark for "Megapod," a self-contained modular data center system bundling AI servers, networking, power distribution, and cooling sold as a single turnkey unit. The filing lands less than a year after Tesla killed Dojo, its in-house training computer, and pushes it into a rack-scale market dominated by Nvidia's GB200 NVL72 and the Dell and Supermicro systems built on it.

title: Mistral OCR 4 Ships Bounding Boxes for 170-Language Document AI
blurb: Mistral released OCR 4, a document extraction API that now returns bounding boxes, block labels, and per-word confidence scores across 170 languages. The model posted a 72% average win rate against competing OCR systems in Mistral's human evaluation and costs $4 per 1,000 pages, with one customer reporting equivalent accuracy at roughly 8x lower cost and 17x lower latency than leading agentic document parsers.

title: See How Sam Altman’s Personal Investments Benefit From Ties to OpenAI
blurb: A Wall Street Journal analysis found that Sam Altman's personal stakes in startups like fusion company Helion have risen sharply after OpenAI explored or finalized deals with those Altman-linked firms. The report adds to scrutiny of conflicts of interest between Altman's private investment portfolio and the partnership and acquisition decisions he steers as OpenAI's CEO.

title: Google tests literature review matrix tool for NotebookLM
blurb: Google is testing a "Lit Review" tool for NotebookLM that turns uploaded sources into a structured comparison matrix, currently in pre-release with no timeline.

title: Superhuman acquires AI detection startup GPTZero
blurb: Superhuman acquired AI detection startup GPTZero, which reached 19M registered users and $30M in annual recurring revenue on just $13.5M raised.

title: HelpBench: Assessing the Ability of LLMs to Provide Privacy, Safety, and Security Advice
blurb: HelpBench is a benchmark of 450 curated questions that tests whether LLMs give accurate digital privacy, safety, and security advice, scored by rubrics and an auto-rater across 18 state-of-the-art models. Models averaged 82%, but one in ten responses scored below 65%, reflecting inaccurate and sometimes harmful guidance on tasks like account recovery, scam detection, and two-factor authentication trade-offs.

title: Maestro Order: A Model-Agnostic Orchestration Harness
blurb: Maestro Order is a model-agnostic orchestration harness that wraps black-box solvers in four primitives (decompose, ensemble, verify, recurse) plus a budget-aware controller that allocates compute to the stages with the highest reliability gain per dollar. A Monte Carlo simulation shows verification amplifies reliability geometrically, lifting accuracy from 0.55 to 0.98 with two gates and 0.999 with four, while voting helps only above chance and saturates on shared errors.

title: Preliminary Thoughts On The Midjourney Scanner
blurb: Midjourney announced a pivot from AI image generation to medical imaging with the Midjourney Scanner, a water tank ringed with ultrasound emitters that reconstructs 3D ultrasound tomography in twenty seconds without radiation. The author, a psychiatrist, argues the SF AI narrative has outrun the medical facts: ultrasound can't penetrate bone or air, ruling out the brain, lungs, and bowels that most CTs and MRIs target. The realistic niche is low-risk screening, whose value remains unproven.

title: Enable Real-Time AI for High-Speed Data Acquisition with DAQIRI
blurb: NVIDIA released DAQIRI (Data Acquisition for Integrated Real-time Instruments), a high-performance networking library in the Holoscan Platform that connects high-bandwidth streaming detectors directly to the NVIDIA software stack. It lets developers run filtering, inference, compression, and event selection in stream over a ConnectX NIC, replacing the traditional collect-store-analyze pipeline that breaks down at rates like LCLS-II's 1 MHz output.

title: My Vibe Coding Adventure, The App and the Experience, Ten Takeaways
blurb: The author recounts vibe coding an app he plans to use regularly, then distills the experience into ten takeaways. The piece covers what worked and what didn't in building a personally useful tool through natural-language prompting, offering practical lessons on where AI-assisted development currently delivers and where it still requires hands-on intervention.

title: OpenAI's new GPT-5.5-Cyber tops Claude Mythos 5 in vulnerability benchmark
blurb: OpenAI launched the full version of GPT-5.5-Cyber, a specialized cybersecurity model for verified defenders that scored 85.6% on the CyberGym vulnerability benchmark, ahead of Claude Mythos 5 at 83.8% and base GPT-5.5 at 81.8%. The release accompanies an expanded Daybreak initiative, including an updated Codex Security plugin that validates findings and generates codebase-specific patches, plus a partner program with Cisco, Cloudflare, CrowdStrike, and Palo Alto Networks.

title: PixJail: Self-Evolving Paper-to-Pipeline Reproduction for Text-to-Image Jailbreak Evaluation
blurb: PixJail is a self-evolving agent framework that reproduces text-to-image jailbreak evaluations directly from a paper and optional reference code, treating the task as a pipeline-level problem spanning prompt transformation, image generation, safety filtering, and multimodal judging. It reproduced eleven representative T2I jailbreak methods, including code-unavailable ones, recovering prior results with 2.1% average error and 0% median error.

title: OpenAI Codex bombards SSDs with needless write operations, costing millions
blurb: OpenAI's Codex coding agent has been writing excessive, redundant data to local SSDs through a clumsy logging implementation that stores information without regard for storage cost or drive wear. The behavior can prematurely exhaust the write endurance of consumer SSDs, and at scale across many users it adds up to millions of dollars in unnecessary hardware degradation.

title: AI PACs pour $20 million into New York Democratic primary in AI regulation battle
blurb: Two super PACs backed by AI companies have spent over $20 million on a single New York Democratic House primary, turning the race into a proxy fight over federal AI regulation. Public First Action, funded by Anthropic, put $11 million behind AI safety advocate Alex Bores, while Leading the Future, backed by Andreessen Horowitz, Greg Brockman, and Perplexity, spent $8 million opposing him.

title: Anthropic reimagines Claude in Slack as nosy, always-on agentic AI coworker
blurb: Anthropic redesigned Claude in Slack as an always-on agentic coworker that monitors channels and proactively surfaces information rather than waiting to be prompted. The reworked integration lets Claude take actions across the workspace, drawing on conversation context to flag relevant items and run tasks autonomously inside the messaging platform.

title: Exabeam launches Praxen, an open-source tool to verify AI agent behavior
blurb: Exabeam released Praxen, an open-source tool under the Apache 2.0 license that verifies an AI agent's behavior before deployment by checking its real tools, configurations, memory, and integrations against a defined policy contract. The tool implements Exabeam's Agent Behavior Verification discipline, flagging where an agent's actual capabilities diverge from its assigned role, recommending fixes, and assigning a security maturity score.

title: ‘The claims in the suit are false’: Workday hits back amid lawsuit claiming AI recruitment discrimination
blurb: A US District judge ruled that a class action accusing Workday's AI candidate-screening software of discriminating by age, race, and disability can proceed, making it the first case to test the legality of automated hiring tools. Workday called the claims false and said its tools do not make hiring decisions, while the judge declined to drop an allegation that the system filters candidates using proxy indicators like employment gaps.

title: London-based Isometric, which develops AI agents that work with human verifiers to automate industrial certification, raised a €34M Series A led by AVP (David Cendon Garcia/EU-Startups)
blurb: Isometric, a London-based startup building AI agents that pair with human verifiers to automate industrial certification, raised a €34M Series A led by AVP. The company targets compliance-heavy certification workflows, keeping humans in the loop to validate agent output while cutting the manual overhead of industrial sign-off.

title: Cerebras Drops After 2026 Sales Outlook Disappoints Investors
blurb: Cerebras shares fell about 10% in late trading after the newly public chipmaker issued a 2026 sales forecast that came in below investor expectations. The outlook underscored the pressure on would-be Nvidia rivals, who must clear the high revenue-growth and profitability bars that a small group of AI chipmakers have set by repeatedly beating Wall Street estimates.

title: As AI Companies Race for Power, Amazon and Google Have the Lead
blurb: Amazon and Google have pulled ahead of rivals in securing the power needed to run AI data centers, according to the WSJ. Amazon benefits from an incumbent advantage in existing energy contracts and infrastructure, while Google stands out for more innovative sourcing approaches as the industry's electricity demands continue to climb.

title: Dirac-Frenkel dynamics with inertia for nonlinearly parametrized solutions of evolution problems
blurb: This paper proposes adding inertia to Dirac-Frenkel dynamics, which determine how nonlinearly parametrized solutions like neural networks or mixture models evolve over time. For redundant parametrizations, standard parameter dynamics can be non-unique or ill-conditioned. The inertial formulation lets velocity information persist in weakly informed directions while well-informed directions still follow the standard dynamics. The authors prove the method is well-posed, provide a posteriori error bounds, and show improved robustness in numerical experiments.

title: Anthropic launches Claude Tag on Team and Enterprise plans
blurb: Anthropic launched Claude Tag, a Slack-based agent that teams summon by typing @claude in a channel to read thread context, run connected tools, and continue multi-step work over hours or days. The beta runs on Opus 4.8 for Enterprise and Team customers, replaces the older Claude in Slack app on August 3, and uses an agent-identity model with channel-scoped permissions and org-level billing.

title: Meta launches new AI glasses in 26 styles and Muse Spark multimodal capabilties
blurb: Meta announced a new line of AI glasses built with EssilorLuxottica, starting at $299 and available in 26 styles across the Meta Adventurer, Meta Fury, and Kylie Jenner frames. The glasses ship with Meta AI powered by Muse Spark, the first multimodal model from Meta Superintelligence Labs, which also rolls out to existing Ray-Ban Meta and Oakley Meta glasses in the US and Canada.

title: FLUX3D: High-Fidelity 3D Gaussian Generation with Diffusion-Aligned Sparse Representation
blurb: FLUX3D is an image-to-3D Gaussian Splatting framework that targets two bottlenecks in sparse-voxel methods: discriminative 2D features that suppress reconstructive detail, and diffusion transformers that poorly align dense 2D tokens with sparse 3D voxels. It pairs Diffusion-Aligned Structured Latents with a sparse-structure-aware diffusion model using a multimodal transformer and modal-aware rotary positional embeddings, outperforming prior state-of-the-art on appearance fidelity.

title: Dell and Nvidia deepen AI factory love-in with high-density PowerEdge servers
blurb: Dell released the PowerEdge XE8812, a liquid-cooled rack server co-engineered with Nvidia that scales to 144 GPUs per rack on Vera Rubin NVL4.

title: A Physics-Informed Fourier-Wavelet Transformer for Multiscale Computational Fluid Dynamics Surrogate Modeling
blurb: This paper presents a physics-informed Fourier-wavelet transformer for next-step velocity-field reconstruction in computational fluid dynamics, combining hybrid spectral encoding with physics-biased self-attention derived from PDE residual diagnostics. On the fluid-structure-interaction benchmark, it reaches an all-channel normalized mean-squared error of 2.70x10^-4 versus 4.02x10^-4 for the strongest baseline, with stronger recovery of localized near-body and wake-core structures.

title: OrbitForge: Text-to-3D Scene Generation via Reconstruction-Anchored Video Synthesis
blurb: OrbitForge is an adapter that converts a single text-generated video into a closed-orbit 3D Gaussian Splatting scene, using frozen video priors and per-prompt reconstruction optimization without task-specific fine-tuning or score-distillation. It builds an initial reconstruction via Deformable Gaussian Splatting, detects missing viewpoints along a prescribed orbit, and regenerates only those views, reaching a 359-degree median span on a 300-prompt T3Bench-derived audit.

title: ASALT: Adaptive State Alignment for Lateral Transfer in Multi-agent Reinforcement Learning
blurb: ASALT is a multi-agent reinforcement learning method that transfers knowledge between source and target domains with mismatched observation and state-space dimensions, which prior transfer approaches required to be identical. It uses observation-level and state-level adapters to map target observations and global states into a shared embedding space, improving sample efficiency and cooperative return while mitigating negative transfer, though gains depend on the degree of domain mismatch.

title: The Warrant Gap: Claim-Conditioned Re-scoring for Fact-Checking
blurb: SIFT introduces claim-conditioned re-scoring of extracted evidence spans against the full claim, addressing how LLM fact-checkers output Supports labels whose cited evidence does not actually license the claim. Paired with WSP, an NLI check that the warrant entails the claim, SIFT recovers up to 27.6 accuracy points lost to naive decomposition across FEVER, SciFact, 5PILS, and DP, while WSP calibrates against human gold evidence at 0.92 AUC and 0.98 precision.

title: Privacy-Preserving RAG via Multi-Agent Semantic Rewriting: Achieving Confidentiality Without Compromising Contextual Fidelity
blurb: This paper introduces a multi-agent framework that sanitizes retrieved content for RAG systems by using three specialized agents for privacy extraction, semantic analysis, and reconstruction to strip sensitive identifiers while preserving meaning. On the ChatDoctor and Wiki-PII datasets, it cut targeted information exposure in LLaMA-3-8B from 144 instances to 1 and reached a BLEU-1 score of 0.122, running as offline preprocessing that adds no inference latency.

title: Building TREX: Code execution and artifact generation for AI code review
blurb: Greptile built TREX (Test, Run, Execute), an execution layer for AI code review that runs the code under review instead of only reading the diff. The system catches runtime bugs that static analysis misses, like logic errors requiring specific state, post-load UI regressions, and race conditions, and shares the main Greptile reviewer's context rather than running as a separate agent to avoid duplicated exploration and wasted compute.

title: Why cloud native belongs at the heart of agentic AI: Lessons from building a multi-agent security platform on Kubernetes
blurb: The author argues that cloud native infrastructure is the right foundation for production agentic AI, drawing on a multi-agent security platform being built at Orange Innovation on Kubernetes. Each agent runs as its own Deployment with isolated resource limits and identity, coordinated via the A2A protocol and LangGraph, with Falco and eBPF feeding syscall events through Kafka into an anomaly model that pre-filters before the LLM-driven agents act.

title: Agent Auth: A lawyer’s day in court
blurb: This post frames AI agent authentication through the analogy of a lawyer representing a client in court. It maps four requirements onto courtroom steps: agent identity (proving who the agent is), principal identity (which user it acts for), delegation via On-Behalf-Of (OBO) tokens carrying scoped permissions, and policy enforcement that validates each action against applicable rules. The argument is that agents are microservices-plus, demanding stronger auth, policy, and observability than traditional services.

title: Themis: An explainable AI-enabled framework for Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback
blurb: Themis is an open framework that combines explainability and human feedback for testing and evaluating Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback, supporting over 200 standard environments and configurable for RL, transparency, and alignment experiments. The authors show it can train reward models that match or beat the environment's true reward signal, and pair it with an auto-scalable cloud platform that handles a thousand users in back-to-back experiments on a modest commercial machine.

title: Cryptographic certificates of validity for trustworthy AI
blurb: This paper proposes cryptographic certificates of validity for agentic AI, compiling a correctness or policy condition into a logical predicate and using a succinct proof system, optionally zero-knowledge, to certify that the condition holds. The approach lets an agent attach an independently checkable proof that its action satisfies an agreed policy, without the verifier trusting the agent or re-executing the computation, sitting between formal verification and cryptographic authentication.

title: Microsoft’s Satya Nadella: We Can’t Let AI Giants Eat the Economy
blurb: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella argued that AI companies must earn society's permission to operate rather than concentrate economic power among a few dominant players. He warned against a future where AI giants capture outsized shares of the economy, framing broad value creation and public trust as preconditions for the technology's long-term legitimacy.

title: Sources: Zhipu is considering a multibillion-dollar Hong Kong share sale after its stock surged 2,000% since its January IPO, pushing its market cap above $128B (Dong Cao/Bloomberg)
blurb: Zhipu is considering a multibillion-dollar Hong Kong share sale after its stock surged 2,000% since its January IPO, lifting its market cap above $128B. The Chinese AI startup, one of the country's leading large language model developers, would tap public markets again to fund expansion amid intensifying competition with rivals like DeepSeek and Alibaba.

title: Sorry, Slackbot. Claude is taking your job
blurb: Anthropic released Claude Tag (@Claude) in research preview, an always-on Slack assistant with a single shared identity that anyone in a channel can summon by typing @Claude. Admins control its access to tools and data per channel, and an optional "ambient" mode lets it proactively surface relevant information and follow up on stalled tasks. It is launching first for Claude Enterprise and Team customers.

title: Tencent Testing New AI Agent for WeChat Workplace App
blurb: Tencent began rolling out Dayuan, an AI agent built on DeepSeek's V4 model, to select users of WeCom, the enterprise version of WeChat. The natural-language assistant marks Tencent's push to lock corporate users into its ecosystem as Chinese tech giants compete to embed AI agents across their workplace platforms.

title: Superhuman has acquired AI authenticity service GPTZero
blurb: Superhuman acquired AI-detection service GPTZero to fold hallucination and plagiarism checks into its Superhuman Go assistant, with teachers and students as the priority audience.

title: UK funds £60M AI labs to challenge US tech dominance with open-source models
blurb: The UK government awarded £60M to Oxford and University College London to build two academic AI research labs focused on open-source, low-hardware alternatives to US models. The six-year funding, up from an initial £40M for a single lab, aims to redesign core AI architectures and reduce Britain's reliance on a handful of US tech firms, with £2M per lab reserved for hiring doctoral students.

title: Qualcomm is about to spend $4B on a startup that makes no chips — because Nvidia’s real moat was never the silicon, and Cristiano Amon finally figured out the shortcut
blurb: Qualcomm is in talks to acquire Modular Inc. for roughly $4B, a startup that builds AI compiler and inference software rather than chips, according to a June 22 Bloomberg report. Modular's MAX framework and Mojo language, created by LLVM and Swift author Chris Lattner, let models run across different silicon without rewriting, giving Qualcomm a path to challenge Nvidia's CUDA lock-in as it builds out a data center business.

title: FlowPipe: LLM-Enhanced Conditional Generative Flow Networks for Data Preparation Pipeline Construction
blurb: FlowPipe is a framework that formulates data preparation pipeline synthesis as conditional probabilistic flow generation over a directed acyclic graph, using Conditional Generative Flow Networks with a Trajectory Balance objective to tie terminal validation rewards back to early operator decisions. Across 74 real-world datasets in two benchmark suites, it outperforms state-of-the-art Multi-DQN baselines, improving accuracy by 11.96% on average and converging 12.5x faster during training.

title: Robinhood launches agentic trading via MCP server for all customers
blurb: Robinhood launched agentic trading through a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server, making it available to all customers. The integration lets AI agents connect directly to Robinhood accounts to execute trades and access portfolio data, extending agent-driven workflows into retail brokerage for the first time at this scale.

title: Only half of US datacenter capacity planned for 2026 is actually  under construction
blurb: A report found that only about half of the US datacenter capacity slated to come online in 2026 is currently under construction, suggesting a wide gap between announced buildout plans and physical reality. The shortfall raises questions about whether projected AI compute supply will materialize on schedule, even as demand forecasts and capital commitments continue to climb.

title: Why the Human Genome's Tangled Physicality May Confound AI
blurb: The author argues that the human genome resists the kind of understanding biologists once expected, since barely 2% of its 3 billion bases code for genes and the real difficulty lies in how those genes are regulated.
